questions.q: Who killed Kumbhakarna in the great war at Lanka?
questions.explanation: Rama himself slew Kumbhakarna on the battlefield with celestial arrows.

questions.q: Kumbhakarna was the brother of which demon king?
questions.explanation: Kumbhakarna was the younger brother of Ravana, the king of Lanka.

questions.q: What was Kumbhakarna most famous for in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Kumbhakarna was known for sleeping for months at a time due to a curse.

questions.q: Kumbhakarna was renowned for his enormous what?
questions.explanation: Kumbhakarna was a colossal giant of immense size and strength.

questions.q: In which war did Kumbhakarna die?
options: The war between Rama and Ravana at Lanka
questions.explanation: Kumbhakarna fell in the war between Rama's forces and Ravana at Lanka.

questions.q: Why was Kumbhakarna woken from his sleep?
questions.explanation: Ravana roused Kumbhakarna from his deep sleep to fight in the war against Rama.

questions.q: On whose side did Kumbhakarna fight in the war?
questions.explanation: Kumbhakarna fought for his brother Ravana against Rama's army.

questions.q: What army did Rama lead against Lanka?
options: An army of vanaras (monkeys) and bears
questions.explanation: Rama's army was composed mainly of vanaras (monkeys) and bears who fought against the rakshasas.

questions.q: What did Kumbhakarna do to many warriors on the battlefield before his death?
questions.explanation: Kumbhakarna wrought havoc, devouring and crushing scores of warriors before Rama slew him.

questions.q: Which weapon did Rama use to kill Kumbhakarna?
questions.explanation: Rama severed Kumbhakarna's limbs and head using celestial arrows from his bow.

questions.q: Who were the parents of Kumbhakarna?
questions.explanation: Kumbhakarna was the son of the sage Vishrava and the rakshasi Kaikesi, making him a half-brother lineage of brahmin and rakshasa.

questions.q: When Brahma granted Kumbhakarna a boon, what did he ask for that became a curse due to Saraswati altering his tongue?
questions.explanation: Kumbhakarna intended to ask for Indrasana but, with Saraswati on his tongue, asked for Nidrasana, thus winning eternal sleep.

questions.q: How was Kumbhakarna typically awakened from his deep sleep, according to tradition?
options: By elephants, loud noises, and food piled before him
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that elephants were driven over him, loud instruments were sounded, and mountains of food were placed to rouse the sleeping giant.

questions.q: Before agreeing to fight, what advice did Kumbhakarna give to Ravana?
options: To return Sita to Rama and make peace
questions.explanation: Kumbhakarna censured Ravana's actions and advised returning Sita, yet loyally chose to fight for his brother anyway.

questions.q: Which vanara did Kumbhakarna seize and carry off during the battle before being freed?
questions.explanation: Kumbhakarna seized Sugriva, the vanara king, and carried him off, though Sugriva later escaped by biting off his ears and nose.

questions.q: How did Sugriva escape from Kumbhakarna's grip?
options: By biting off his ears and nose
questions.explanation: Sugriva tore off Kumbhakarna's ears and nose with his teeth and nails, then leaped free to rejoin the fight.

questions.q: What did Rama do first before finally beheading Kumbhakarna?
options: Severed his arms and legs with arrows
questions.explanation: Rama first cut off Kumbhakarna's arms and legs with his arrows before severing his great head.

questions.q: Which celestial weapon (astra) is traditionally said to have beheaded Kumbhakarna?
questions.explanation: Rama invoked a weapon presided over by Indra (the Indrastra) to sever Kumbhakarna's head.

questions.q: What weapon did Kumbhakarna chiefly wield in the battle against Rama?
options: A massive iron mace or club (shula/parigha)
questions.explanation: Kumbhakarna fought wielding an enormous club and spear, smashing through the vanara ranks.

questions.q: Where did Kumbhakarna's severed head and body fall after his death?
options: Into the sea and onto the battlefield, crushing rakshasas
questions.explanation: His huge head fell crushing structures and his body toppled, with parts crashing into the sea, killing many around.

questions.q: How did Ravana react upon hearing of Kumbhakarna's death?
questions.explanation: Ravana was overcome with grief and swooned upon learning that his mighty brother Kumbhakarna had been slain.

questions.q: Kumbhakarna's son, who also fought and died in the war, was named what?
questions.explanation: Kumbhakarna's sons Kumbha and Nikumbha also fought in the war and were slain by the vanaras.

questions.q: According to the Valmiki Ramayana, for how long did Kumbhakarna typically sleep before waking briefly?
options: Six months asleep, then one day awake
One year asleep, then a month awake
Three months asleep, then a week awake
questions.explanation: By Brahma's regulation of the boon, Kumbhakarna slept for six months and remained awake for only a single day.

questions.q: Why did Brahma originally grant Kumbhakarna the boon of long sleep rather than a more destructive power?
options: Because the gods feared he would devour the three worlds if always awake
questions.explanation: The gods, fearing Kumbhakarna's voracious appetite would consume the worlds, had Saraswati twist his tongue so he asked for sleep instead of Indra's throne.

questions.q: Which rakshasa general's earlier death in the war prompted Ravana to finally awaken Kumbhakarna?
questions.explanation: After his commander Prahasta and others were slain, a desperate Ravana ordered Kumbhakarna to be awakened to turn the tide.

questions.q: In the Valmiki Ramayana, what did Kumbhakarna say about the proper conduct (dharma) of ministers even as he agreed to fight?
options: That ministers should always flatter the king
That good counsel should be given before action, but he would still fight for his brother out of loyalty
That he alone should rule Lanka
questions.explanation: Kumbhakarna rebuked Ravana for not heeding wise counsel earlier, yet declared he would loyally fight and die for his brother regardless.

questions.q: Which of Rama's allies, Ravana's own brother, had foretold disaster and defected before Kumbhakarna's death?
questions.explanation: Vibhishana, the righteous brother of Ravana and Kumbhakarna, had already defected to Rama and warned of Lanka's doom.

questions.q: When Kumbhakarna charged the battlefield, how did the panicked vanara army initially respond?
options: Many fled in terror until Angada and others rallied them
questions.explanation: The vanaras fled in terror at Kumbhakarna's monstrous onslaught until Angada and other leaders rallied them back to fight.

questions.q: Before Rama killed him, which arrows is Rama described as using to first cut off Kumbhakarna's hand holding the weapon?
options: Vayavya astra (wind weapon) to sever the arm
questions.explanation: Rama loosed the Vayavya (wind) weapon to sever Kumbhakarna's mace-bearing arm before finishing him with the Indra weapon.

questions.q: What is the deeper traditional significance often attributed to Kumbhakarna's death in the Ramayana narrative?
options: It marked the end of the war
It was a turning point showing Ravana's doom was near, breaking his strongest defense
questions.explanation: The fall of the mighty Kumbhakarna, Ravana's strongest warrior after Indrajit, signaled that Ravana's defeat and doom were now inevitable.
